translations.*EX: 第一版上線(1.0.0) -&gt; 補強第一版的功能 (1.1.0): *EX: First release (1.0.0) -> Enhance features of the first version (1.1.0) -> Found issues to fix (1.1.1) -> Found more issues (1.1.2) -> Continue enhancing first version features (1.2.0) -> Major update (2.0.0) -> Found issues to fix (2.0.1) … and so on*
translations.*一般習慣使用日期、number（每個新版本都從 0 開始），並搭配 CI/fa: *It is common to use dates or numbers (starting from 0 for each new version) along with CI/fastlane to automatically increment the build number during packaging.*
translations.*一般習慣使用語意化版本 x.y.z 或 x.y。*\n\n: *It is common practice to use semantic versioning x.y.z or x.y.*
translations.*先比 主版號，主版號 等於時 再比 次版號，次版號 等於時 再比 修訂號。*\: *First compare the major version. If the major versions are equal, then compare the minor version. If the minor versions are also equal, then compare the patch version.*
translations.- For App Store and external identificat:
- For App Store and external identification use

- Property List Key: `CFBundleShortVersionString`

- Content can only contain numbers and "."

- The official recommendation is to use the semantic versioning format x.y.z

- 2020121701, 2.0, and 2.0.0.1 are all acceptable  
  (The summary table below shows the naming conventions of App versions on the App Store)

- No more than 18 characters

- The format may be incorrect; you can build & run but cannot upload the package to the App Store.

- Can only increment upwards, cannot repeat, cannot decrease
translations.- Must be a non-negative integer\n\n- No:
- Must be a non-negative integer

- No zero padding

- 0.y.z indicates the initial development stage and should not be used for official release versions.

- Incrementing Numerically
translations.- Used internally during development and:
- Used internally during development and for stage identification; not exposed to users.

- Used for packaging and uploading to the App Store (the same build number cannot be uploaded repeatedly).

- Property List Key: `CFBundleVersion`

- Content can only consist of numbers and "."

- The official recommendation is to use the semantic versioning format x.y.z

- 1, 2020121701, 2.0, and 2.0.0.1 are all acceptable.

- No more than 18 characters

- The format may be incorrect; you can build & run but cannot upload the package to the App Store.

- The same APP version number cannot be duplicated, but different APPs can have the same version number.  
  Example: 1.0.0 build: 1.0.0, 1.1.0 build: 1.0.0 ✅
translations.- x: Major version (major): for signific:
- x: Major version (major): for significant updates (multiple page interface redesigns, major feature releases)

- y: Minor version: for optimizing or enhancing existing features (adding small features under major functions)

- z: Patch number: used when fixing bugs in the current version
translations.- x: Major version (major): when you mak:
- x: Major version (major): when you make incompatible API changes

- y: Minor version number: when you add backward-compatible new features

- z: Patch number: when you make backward-compatible bug fixes

translations.Regex 是參考「 [語意化版本文件](https://semver.org/: The regex is modified based on the reference provided in the [Semantic Versioning document](https://semver.org/lang/zh-TW/#%E6%9C%89%E5%BB%BA%E8%AD%B0%E7%94%A8%E6%96%BC%E6%AA%A2%E6%9F%A5%E8%AA%9E%E6%84%8F%E5%8C%96%E7%89%88%E6%9C%AC%E7%9A%84%E6%AD%A3%E8%A6%8F%E8%A1%A8%E7%A4%BA%E5%BC%8Fregex%E5%97%8E){:target="_blank"}.
translations.[How to compare two app version strings: [How to compare two app version strings in Swift](https://sarunw.com/posts/how-to-compare-two-app-version-strings-in-swift/){:target="_blank"}
translations.```\nInput: 1.0.0 => Output: .unSpecifie:
```
Input: 1.0.0 => Output: .unSpecified, Version(1.0.0)
Input: ≥ 1.0.1 => Output: .higherThanOrEqual, Version(1.0.0)
```
translations.```swift\nlet version = \"1.0.0\"\nprint:
```swift
let version = "1.0.0"
print(version.compare("1.0.0", options: .numeric) == .orderedSame) // true 1.0.0 = 1.0.0
print(version.compare("1.22.0", options: .numeric) == .orderedAscending) // true 1.0.0 < 1.22.0
print(version.compare("0.0.9", options: .numeric) == .orderedDescending) // true 1.0.0 > 0.0.9
print(version.compare("2", options: .numeric) == .orderedAscending) // true 1.0.0 < 2
```
translations.x: 主版號 (major)：有重大更新時（多個頁面介面翻新、主打功能上線）\n: x: Major version (major): for significant updates (multiple page interface redesigns, major feature releases)
translations.x: 主版號 (major)：當你做了不相容的 API 修改\n: x: Major version (major): when you make incompatible API changes
translations.y: 次版號 (minor)：現有功能優化、補強時（大功能下的小功能新增）\n: y: Minor version: for optimizing or enhancing existing features (adding small features under major functions)
translations.y: 次版號 (minor)：當你做了向下相容的功能性新增\n: y: Minor version number: when you add backward-compatible new features
translations.z: 修訂號 (patch)：修正目前版本的 bug時\n: z: Patch number: used when fixing bugs in the current version
translations.z: 修訂號 (patch)：當你做了向下相容的問題修正\n: z: Patch number: when you make backward-compatible bug fixes
translations.⚠️但需注意若遇到格式不同要判斷相同是會有誤：\n\n: ⚠️ However, be aware that comparing versions with different formats as equal can cause errors:
translations.一般如果是緊急修復(Hot Fix)才會動到修訂號，正常狀況下都為 0；如果有新: Usually, the patch number is only changed for urgent fixes (Hot Fixes) and is normally set to 0; when a new version is released, it can be reset to 0.
translations.一般還是以 x.y.z 為主。\n\n: Generally, x.y.z is still the main format.
translations.不過目前已經有很完整的 [Version](https://github.com: However, there is already a complete [Version](https://github.com/mrackwitz/Version){:target="_blank"} for handling Pod projects, so there's no need to reinvent the wheel. I just wanted to streamline the setup process XD.

translations.其實就是實現前文所述判斷邏輯，最後開一個 compareWith 的方法口，方便: This actually implements the previously described comparison logic, and finally opens a compareWith method to allow external input of the parsed results for the final judgment.
translations.另外還可在修訂號之後加入「先行版號資訊 (ex: 1.0.1-alpha)」或「: You can also add "pre-release version info (e.g., 1.0.1-alpha)" or "build metadata (e.g., 1.0.0-alpha+001)" after the patch number. However, iOS app versions do not allow these two formats to be uploaded to the App Store, so they will not be discussed here. For details, please refer to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/lang/zh-TW/){:target="_blank"}.
translations.可以看到 Version 就是個 major,minor,patch 的儲存器，: You can see that Version is basically a storage for major, minor, and patch. The parsing method is written as static for easy external use, allowing input like `1.0.0` or `≥1.0.1`. This makes string parsing and config file parsing more convenient.
translations.可直接使用已用輪子： [mrackwitz/Version](https://g: You can directly use an existing library: [mrackwitz/Version](https://github.com/mrackwitz/Version){:target="_blank"} The following is a reimplementation.
translations.同個 APP 版本號下不能重複，反之不同APP 版本號可以重複\nex: 1.0:
The same APP version number cannot be duplicated, but different APPs can have the same version number.  
Example: 1.0.0 build: 1.0.0, 1.1.0 build: 1.0.0 ✅
translations.大於等於此版本但小於此版本的(上一階層版號+1)\n\n: Greater than or equal to this version but less than (the next major version + 1)
translations.官方也是建議使用語意化版本 x.y.z 格式\n: The official recommendation is to use the semantic versioning format x.y.z
translations.實際我們知道 1 == 1.0.0 ，但若用此方式判斷將得到 `.ordered: In practice, we know 1 == 1.0.0, but using this method will result in `.orderedDescending`; you can [refer to this article for padding zeros before comparison](https://sarunw.com/posts/how-to-compare-two-app-version-strings-in-swift/){:target="_blank"}. Normally, once you choose the app version format, it should remain consistent—if you use x.y.z, always use x.y.z, not sometimes x.y.z and sometimes x.y.
translations.所有 iOS APP 開發者都會碰到的兩個數字，Version Number 和: Every iOS app developer encounters two numbers: Version Number and Build Number. Recently, I had a requirement related to version numbers to prompt users for app reviews. Along the way, I explored version number details. At the end of the article, I will also share my complete solution for version number comparison.
translations.打包上傳到 App Store 識別使用（相同 build number 無法重: Used for packaging and uploading to the App Store (the same build number cannot be uploaded repeatedly)
translations.有任何問題及指教歡迎 [與我聯絡](https://www.zhgchg.li/: If you have any questions or feedback, feel free to [contact me](https://www.zhgchg.li/contact){:target="_blank"}.
translations.有時候我們會需要使用版本進行判斷，例如：低於 x.y.z 版本則跳強制更新、等於: Sometimes we need to use versions for decision-making, such as forcing an update if the version is below x.y.z, or prompting for a review if it equals a specific version. In these cases, the ability to compare two version strings is necessary.
translations.格式不合可以 build & run 但無法打包上傳到 App Store\n: The format may be incorrect; you can build & run but cannot upload the package to the App Store.
translations.版本號規則及判斷比較解決方案\n\n: Version Number Rules and Comparison Solution
translations.目前打算將 Version 再進行優化、效能測試調整、整理打包，然後跑一次建立自: Currently planning to further optimize Version, conduct performance testing and adjustments, organize packaging, and then run the process to create my own CocoaPods.
translations.稍微統計了一下排行版上 app 的版本號格式，如上圖。\n\n: I did a brief survey of the version number formats of apps on the leaderboard, as shown in the image above.
translations.複雜方式這邊遵照使用語意化版本 x.y.z 最為格式規範，自行使用 Regex: The complex method follows the semantic versioning format x.y.z, using Regex to parse the string and implementing comparison operators. In addition to the basic =/>/≥/</≤ operators, it also implements the ~> operator (same as Cocoapods version specification) and supports static input.
translations.關於實際使用在 iOS APP 版本控制上，因為我們僅作為 Release AP: Regarding the actual use in iOS APP version control, since we only use it as a marker for Release APP versions and there are no dependencies with other apps or software, the definition in practice is defined by each team. The following are just personal thoughts:
translations.首先介紹「 [語意化版本](https://semver.org/lang/zh: First, let's introduce the [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/lang/zh-TW/){:target="_blank"} specification, which mainly addresses issues in software dependencies and management. For example, in Cocoapods, if I use Moya 4.0, which depends on Alamofire 2.0.0, and Alamofire gets updated—whether with new features, bug fixes, or a complete incompatible overhaul—without a common versioning standard, things can get chaotic because you won't know which versions are compatible or safe to update to.
